case_studies.name: Normal V2X Commuter Network
case_studies.description: Stable vehicular network during regular commute hours. Moderate traffic density, balanced network requirements. No specific security threats detected.

case_studies.name: Byzantine Attack Scenario
case_studies.description: Security monitoring detects anomalous node behavior — ~28% of nodes sending forged location data. Attack risk surges to 85%. Network operators prioritize maximum security over throughput and latency.

case_studies.name: Mass Event Emergency Scale-up
case_studies.description: Post-event dispersal at a major stadium. 400+ autonomous ride-share vehicles activated simultaneously. Transaction rate surges to 7700 TPS (payments, routing, scheduling). Pure throughput scaling problem.

case_studies.name: Energy-Constrained Remote Network
case_studies.description: Remote mountain highway with 200 solar-powered RSU nodes. Energy budget only 25% of nominal capacity. Low traffic volume (60 TPS) but blockchain recording must remain uninterrupted.

case_studies.name: Small Network Instant Finality
case_studies.description: Enclosed smart parking facility with 40 RSU nodes managing 300 parking spots. Each parking command requires absolute finality within 2 seconds (vehicle already in motion). High connectivity (90%), strong consistency requirement (92%).
